title: What am I trying to control about sleep?
subtitle: A journalling set for examining the control habits that prevent sleep.
excerpt: A journal prompt set for understanding the sleep-control patterns: the monitoring, the scheduling, the will to make sleep happen on demand. Five prompts, twenty minutes. Do this in the evening before bed or in the morning if you had a poor night.
sourceNotes: Drawn from Day 4 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025), "Let go of control."

practice.whenToUse: Use in the evening to examine what you are doing to try to manage sleep, or the morning after a night where monitoring kept you awake.

content.text: A journal set for the control habits that work against sleep. Five prompts. Allow twenty minutes.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What does your relationship with sleep feel like right now?
Tense? Resigned? Hopeful? Write what's true.
What do you do in the hour before bed that is meant to help you sleep?
List the habits. Which ones are about control and which ones are truly restful?
What specifically do you try to control once you're in bed?
Monitoring yourself for tiredness, checking the clock, calculating hours. Name the specific habit.
What are you afraid will happen if you stop trying to control it?
The fear underneath the habit. Write it plainly.
What would you do tonight if you truly believed sleep would arrive without your effort?
Name one specific thing you would stop doing or do differently.
Drawn from Day 4 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025), "Let go of control."
